exam_format: Computer-based or paper test at DMV office; closed-book

testing_centers.notes: Walk-in or appointment at local DMV/motor vehicle office; no third-party testing center.

id_requirements: Valid proof of identity, Social Security number, and proof of residency required. Requirements vary by state. Acceptable documents typically include a birth certificate or passport (identity), Social Security card or W-2 (SSN), and utility bill or bank statement (residency). REAL ID-compliant licenses require additional documentation.
exam_rules: Closed-book written test on traffic laws, road signs, and safe driving practices. Calculators and personal items not permitted. Most states allow 2–3 free attempts; additional fees may apply after failures. Test administered at local DMV office on a computer terminal or paper. Most states require in-person testing; a small number offer online options for renewals.
renewal_options: License renewal required every 4–8 years depending on state. Renewal typically does not require re-taking the written test unless the license has been expired for an extended period (usually 1–4 years, varies by state). Many states offer online or mail-in renewal for standard licenses.

retake_policy.notes: Most states require a 1-day wait between attempts. Some states allow same-day retake. Fees may apply for retakes after the first 2–3 attempts.

official_study_resources.title: State Driver's Handbook
official_study_resources.notes: Each state publishes a free official driver's handbook covering all test topics. Available at the state DMV website or dmv.org.

official_study_resources.title: DMV Practice Tests
official_study_resources.notes: Free unofficial practice tests organized by state at dmv.org.

aliases: washington driver s license knowledge test
